action.reasoning: Observation: test_preprocess.py fully succeeded (FULL SEQUENTIAL PREPROCESS with Pi0NewLineProcessor + TokenizerProcessorStep handling, language merge after mutation, observation/images/state preserved, PIL 224x224 under exact base/left_wrist/right_wrist keys, padded 32D state/action, complementary_data+task, dummy_action; all 6 processors complete without error). test_obs.py reaches mock success but predict.py is still truncated at _safe_preprocess causing 'All image features are missing from the batch' ValueError inside policy.predict_action_chunk -> fallback zero-action (all 22+ prior evals 0.0 success_rate). RecordingEvaluator, PI0 load, processors list, delta bridge, chunking, and mock are solid. Analysis: Exact working batch+deepcopy+language-merge-after-NewLine/Tokenizer pattern from diagnostic resolves the training-oriented pipeline mutation issue (partial dict return from language steps). Full non-truncated predict.py copying this pattern exactly into _safe_preprocess + _prepare_observation (map rgb[0,2,3] to base/left/right_wrist as PIL per history, state pad, dummy_action=32D), policy.predict_action_chunk+postprocess+pending queue (amortizes cost), tuned _action_to_ee delta bridge, complete main() with eval_config.json parsing + track_path + exact JSON episodes output, and full RecordingEvaluator (super init + evaluate_single_episode per contract) will eliminate fallback and produce real PI0 actions. Matches intended Pi0 track (LIBERO-pretrained on similar pick/place should yield >0 on add_condiment/select once end-to-end; first non-zero baseline expected). Decision: Overwrite predict.py with COMPLETE clean non-truncated implementation (exact diagnostic pattern + full methods + error recovery only on true exceptions, improved view mapping, chunking, no syntax issues). Keep test files. Run python test_preprocess.py && python test_obs.py for validation (expect FULL SEQUENTIAL PREPROCESS SUCCEEDED + real policy actions in mock without fallback/image-missing). Then submit_eval on base checkpoint for first non-zero success_rate before LoRA/training.
